mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns turn positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is not rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with non-increasing FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL tightens real financial conditions and raises the probability of policy restraint, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days after an inflation acceleration.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if CPI shocks do not coincide with weaker returns than the unconditional sample.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is already elevated and PAYEMS is still falling, labor income growth is deteriorating and earnings risk increases, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 9% and PAYEMS falls month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined trigger does not improve downside precision versus either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and inventory replenishment, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive INDPRO momentum do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than months with flat or negative momentum, or if Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumer spending, which lifts revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises by more than 0.5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS acceleration fails to raise the share of positive 20-day SPX returns above 55%, or if the effect disappears in the next rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in HOUST and GDPC1 marks a housing-led demand contraction that weakens cyclical profits, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and GDPC1 both decline versus their prior observations
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in HOUST and GDPC1 do not predict weaker 60-day SPX returns than either decline alone, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 40% positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE during a monetary tightening cycle confirms labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-market deterioration during tightening does not produce more negative 20-day SPX returns than the same UNRATE rise during easing cycles, or if the triggered signal's hit rate falls below 50% negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO continues to recover after a recessionary trough, the market prices cyclical earnings improvement and S&P 500 returns turn positive over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-trough INDPRO recoveries do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal becomes negative in more than one rolling 18-month window.
